input.background: A $t$ test calculates $t_{\text {stat }}=6.60$. Assuming the study had more than just a few observations, you do not need a $t$ table or software utility to draw a conclusion about the test.
input.question: Why is a look-up table unnecessary?

output.answer: We can say that the $t$ test statistic is in the far right-hand tail of the sampling distribution and the $P$-value will be very, very small (e.g., less than 0.01).
